title: salix myrsinites subsp. breviserrata
intro: Salix myrsinites subsp. breviserrata, also known as the alpine willow, is a subspecies of the willow plant, typically found in mountainous regions, characterized by its small, serrated leaves and slender branches. The alpine willow has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and even alleviating symptoms of respiratory problems such as bronchitis. Alpine willow can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
